title: biscutella prealpina
intro: Biscutella prealpina, also known as the Alps kidney-vetch, is a plant species belonging to the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its small white flowers and kidney-shaped seed pods. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and saponins. Biscutella prealpina is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as a natural antioxidant. It can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including hypertension and kidney stones.
